title: RIOT Engineering Division and Capital Efficiency Strategy
text: RIOT's acquisition of ESS Metron, an electrical component engineering and manufacturing firm catering to bitcoin miners, for approximately $50 million in 2021 has proven strategically valuable for improving capital efficiency. The engineering division has delivered significant cost savings, with the company reporting $23 million in capital expenditure savings since the acquisition through December 2024. In Q1 2026, Engineering revenue reached $22.2 million, up 59.7% year-over-year from $13.9 million in the prior-year quarter. The division's backlog reached $193.4 million as of Q1 2026, with 90% of the backlog attributed to data center projects, indicating strong forward revenue visibility. The engineering revenue stream provides RIOT with a third revenue pillar alongside bitcoin mining and data center operations. The vertical integration of engineering capabilities has enabled the company to design optimized electrical infrastructure for its own facilities while commercializing engineering services for external customers. The division is also supporting the company's data center buildout, particularly the 112 megawatts of data center construction initiated at Corsicana. The engineering business provides GAAP revenue recognition separate from Bitcoin mining and data center operations, allowing investors to analyze margin profiles for each business segment independently.
